findings.summary: torn-tail glue: recovery onto an entries.jsonl ending in a partial line glued the restored JSON onto it, and the same-pass non-git prune then dropped the wal copy — recovery itself could permanently destroy the entry it exists to save; fixed with a newline-prefix guard, probe-confirmed in both git and non-git variants, torn-tail unit test revert-checked RED

three raw NUL bytes (the pair separator written literally) made src/journal.ts a binary file — the core new module rendered as 'Binary file not shown' in PR diffs, defeating the review gate; replaced with \u0000 source escapes, pinned by a no-NUL source-hygiene test

gitignore migration gap: existing consumers (including this repo) would commit the untracked wal on their next 'git add .vfkb' — the tracked-journal design RFC-034 rejects as circular; fixed for this repo's .gitignore plus a doctor warn when a git brain's .journal/ exists unignored

round-1 minors m4-m9 all addressed: doctor wording no longer claims any command restores; wal rewrites temp+rename with the residual lock-free race documented; unknown-HEAD passes prune nothing; VFKB_NO_JOURNAL disables recovery too; hook e2e test pins the restore note inside additionalContext; writeMeta refresh after restore
m10: journal recovery runs only in the Claude Code session-start hook — pi face and kb_resume never heal (spec-conformant; the vilonotes tier is where incident 3 lives) — tracked as #205
m11 (round 2, new): doctor 'journal gitignore' warn false-positives when the brain lives outside the repo (check-ignore exit 128 read as not-ignored) — cosmetic wrong-advice, tracked as #206
